_ai: AI fills this. Derived from: architecture.json + infrastructure.json + non-functional-requirements.json. Do not edit manually.

dlq.description: Each SQS consumer queue has a paired Dead Letter Queue (DLQ). After maxReceiveCount=4 receive attempts, the message is moved to the DLQ. Platform Operators triage via the ops-service console.

monitoring.alert: ALT003 — sqs_dlq_depth_messages > 0 fires within 5 minutes of first DLQ entry
monitoring.console: ops-service provides a filterable UI: tenant_id, event_type, failure_reason, occurred_at

operatorActions: Inspect full event payload and failure reason in ops console
Identify root cause: malformed payload, transient downstream failure, or permanent error
Correct payload if needed and re-queue to original consumer queue (emits dlq.event_requeued)
Or discard with documented reason — produces dlq.event_discarded and AuditLog entry
Confirm re-queued event is successfully processed via Grafana trace
dlq.idempotencySafety: All consumers are idempotent via event_id watermarks. Re-queuing an already-processed event produces no side effects.

replay.description: The ops-service can replay historical events for a tenant from the event archive, allowing downstream services to rebuild derived state idempotently after bugs, migrations, or data corruption.
triggers: Dashboard corruption detected — rebuild DashboardView from event log
Analytics state corrupted — rebuild stream processing state
Bug fix deployed that changes event processing logic — rebuild affected views
Tenant data migration — reprocess events with updated tenant config

options: Full tenant replay (all events for a tenant_id)

implementation.source: S3 event archive (bookvault-event-archive bucket) or live PostgreSQL outbox table for recent events
implementation.mechanism: ops-service reads events in chronological order and publishes to target service's SQS queue directly (bypassing SNS fan-out to avoid unintended side effects)
implementation.idempotency: All consumers use event_id watermarks. Replaying an already-processed event_id is a no-op. Safe to replay partially-processed ranges.
implementation.liveIsolation: Replay runs as a separate job tracked in ops-service. Does not affect live booking operations (FR011).

jobTracking.visibility: Replay job status visible in ops-service console with event count, progress, and error log

database.schedule: Daily snapshot at 02:00 UTC (during maintenance window)
database.walArchive: WAL continuously streamed to S3 — enables point-in-time recovery to any second within the retention window
database.rto: 4 hours (restore to new RDS instance + validation)
database.crossRegionCopy: Snapshots copied to eu-west-1 (Ireland) daily for disaster recovery

objectStorage.versioning: Enabled on bookvault-exports and bookvault-event-archive buckets
objectStorage.replication: Cross-region replication to eu-west-1 for event archive bucket
objectStorage.retention: Event archive: 90 days hot + Glacier; Export archives: 7 days then deleted

eventArchive.description: All AppointmentEvents exported to S3 (bookvault-event-archive) daily in Parquet format per tenant, partitioned by tenant_id/year/month/day. Used as replay source and long-term audit trail.
eventArchive.rpo: 24 hours (last daily export) — live RDS WAL provides < 1 hour RPO for recent events

severityLevels.description: Complete booking service outage or tenant isolation violation
severityLevels.escalation: On-call engineer immediately; CTO notified within 30 minutes
severityLevels.communicationTemplate: Status page updated within 10 minutes of detection
Internal Slack update; status page updated if customer-facing impact confirmed
Tenant admin notified per analytics alert configuration

incidentResponse.postMortem: Required for all P1 incidents. Template in project-docs/06-operations/. Due within 5 business days.

maintenanceWindows.rationale: Lowest booking volume window across UK target market time zones
plannedActivities: RDS automated backup (02:00 UTC daily — within window on Sundays)
RDS minor version upgrades (pre-announced 7 days in advance)

notificationPolicy.tenantAdmins: Email notification 7 days and 24 hours before any planned downtime window
notificationPolicy.statusPage: Updated 24 hours before planned maintenance; cleared when complete

zeroDowntimeDeployments.mechanism: ECS blue-green deployment via CodeDeploy. New task set spun up; traffic shifted after health checks pass; old tasks drained.
zeroDowntimeDeployments.databaseMigrations: Backward-compatible migrations applied before deployment. Rollback migration available before traffic cut. Schema changes that require locking are applied during maintenance window only.
